name: Xavier
description: Xavier comes from a Basque place name and gained worldwide use through the influence of Saint Francis Xavier.

famousNamesakes.name: Saint Francis Xavier
famousNamesakes.description: Spanish Jesuit missionary and co-founder of the Society of Jesus

name: Xander
description: Typically a shortened form of Alexander, it retains the Greek sense of defending people and has modern international usage.

name: Ximena
description: Medieval Iberian name with debated origin; often linked to Basque or Spanish forms, and historically paired with Jimena.

name: Xiomara
description: Used especially in Hispanic communities, Xiomara is often analyzed as derived from Germanic elements meaning readiness and battle, though details vary.

name: Xzavier
description: Xzavier is an orthographic variant of Xavier, ultimately linked to a Basque place-name meaning new house.

name: Xochitl
description: A Nahuatl name meaning flower, it is culturally significant in Mexico and reflects indigenous linguistic continuity and identity.

name: Xena
description: Xena is associated with the Greek xenos root relating to guest or stranger, and gained modern visibility through popular media.

name: Xenia
description: A Greek name tied to the cultural concept of guest friendship, widely transmitted through Orthodox and European traditions.

name: Xitlali
description: Xitlali is a Nahuatl name meaning star, reflecting indigenous linguistic heritage and contemporary use in the Americas.

name: Xavion
description: Predominantly a modern American formation, it appears influenced by Xavier but lacks a settled historical derivation.

name: Xavi
description: Xavi is a diminutive of Xavier, from Basque toponymy commonly interpreted as new house, widely used in Catalan and Spanish contexts.

name: Xyla
description: Xyla is modeled on Greek xylon meaning wood, and is used as a rare modern name with nature associations.

name: Xitlaly
description: A Nahuatl-origin name meaning star, used in Mexican contexts and reflecting Indigenous linguistic heritage in modern naming.

name: Xavian
description: A modern variant related to Xavier, ultimately from a Basque place name associated with a saintly tradition in Catholic history.

name: Xochilt

name: Xaiden

name: Xavior
description: Spelling variant of Xavier, from Basque place-name meaning new house and associated with Saint Francis Xavier.

name: Xavien

name: Xzavion

name: Xaiver
description: A spelling variant of Xavier, from a Basque place name meaning new house, widely used in Christian naming traditions.

name: Xayden
description: Xayden is a contemporary variant spelling aligned with Zayden patterns, with meaning largely determined by usage rather than etymology.

name: Xavier
description: Xavier comes from a Basque place name and gained worldwide use through the influence of Saint Francis Xavier.

name: Xoe
